title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "The Alchemist" book by Paulo Coelho?
Options:
A. As the boy traveled across the desert, he kept listening to his heart. He started to grasp its dodges and tricks, and to accept it as it was. His fear faded away and he forgot about needing to go back to the oasis. One afternoon, his heart said it was happy, even though it complains sometimes since it belongs to a person, and people's hearts tend to be like that. People are too scared to chase their biggest dreams because they feel unworthy or unable to reach them. Their hearts become frightened just thinking about loved ones who left forever, moments that could've been good but weren't, or treasures buried in the sands that stayed hidden. When this happens, their hearts suffer greatly.
B. As they went across the desert, the boy kept listening to his heart. He started to understand how it worked and to accept it as it was. He lost his fear and forgot about needing to go back to the oasis. One afternoon, his heart told him it was happy, even though it complains sometimes since it's a person's heart and people's hearts are like that. People are scared to chase their biggest dreams because they feel they don't deserve them or can't achieve them. Their hearts become fearful just thinking about loved ones leaving forever, moments that could have been good but weren't, or treasures hidden in the sands that were never found. When these things happen, their hearts suffer terribly.
C. The boy kept listening to his heart as they went through the desert. He began to understand its tricks and accept it for what it was. He stopped being afraid and no longer thought about needing to return to the oasis. One day his heart said it was happy, even though it complains sometimes because it's a person's heart and they tend to be that way. People are too afraid to go after their most important dreams because they feel undeserving or incapable. Their hearts become scared just thinking about loved ones gone forever, moments that could've been good but weren't, or treasures buried in the sand that stayed hidden. When this happens, their hearts suffer immensely.  
D. The boy continued to listen to his heart as they crossed the desert. He came to understand its dodges and tricks, and to accept it as it was. He lost his fear, and forgot about his need to go back to the oasis, because, one afternoon, his heart told him that it was happy. “Even though I complain sometimes,” it said, “it’s because I’m the heart of a person, and people’s hearts are that way. People are afraid to pursue their most important dreams, because they feel that they don’t deserve them, or that they’ll be unable to achieve them. We, their hearts, become fearful just thinking of loved ones who go away forever, or of moments that could have been good but weren’t, or of treasures that might have been found but were forever hidden in the sands. Because, when these things happen, we suffer terribly.” 
Answer:

options.A: As the boy traveled across the desert, he kept listening to his heart. He started to grasp its dodges and tricks, and to accept it as it was. His fear faded away and he forgot about needing to go back to the oasis. One afternoon, his heart said it was happy, even though it complains sometimes since it belongs to a person, and people's hearts tend to be like that. People are too scared to chase their biggest dreams because they feel unworthy or unable to reach them. Their hearts become frightened just thinking about loved ones who left forever, moments that could've been good but weren't, or treasures buried in the sands that stayed hidden. When this happens, their hearts suffer greatly.
options.B: As they went across the desert, the boy kept listening to his heart. He started to understand how it worked and to accept it as it was. He lost his fear and forgot about needing to go back to the oasis. Their hearts become fearful just thinking about loved ones leaving forever, moments that could have been good but weren't, or treasures hidden in the sands that were never found. When these things happen, their hearts suffer terribly.

He stopped being afraid and no longer thought about needing to return to the oasis. One day his heart said it was happy, even though it complains sometimes because it's a person's heart and they tend to be that way. People are too afraid to go after their most important dreams because they feel undeserving or incapable. Their hearts become scared just thinking about loved ones gone forever, moments that could've been good but weren't, or treasures buried in the sand that stayed hidden. When this happens, their hearts suffer immensely.
options.D: The boy continued to listen to his heart as they crossed the desert. He came to understand its dodges and tricks, and to accept it as it was. He lost his fear, and forgot about his need to go back to the oasis, because, one afternoon, his heart told him that it was happy. “Even though I complain sometimes,” it said, “it’s because I’m the heart of a person, and people’s hearts are that way. People are afraid to pursue their most important dreams, because they feel that they don’t deserve them, or that they’ll be unable to achieve them. We, their hearts, become fearful just thinking of loved ones who go away forever, or of moments that could have been good but weren’t, or of treasures that might have been found but were forever hidden in the sands. Because, when these things happen, we suffer terribly.”
